assertions.reason: Using the mission-scoped commit set recorded in progress_log.jsonl, the effective continuation-core path set covers 17 mission commits / 44 unique paths and stays entirely within the updated allow-list, including the intentional deletion of packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/builtin/todowrite.ts.

The same mission-scoped git log/stat stays within the approved allow-list, and a structured scan of actual command fields in progress_log.jsonl and worker-transcripts.jsonl found zero forbidden git verbs; the recorded staging evidence that remains is path-explicit.

frictions: A raw git diff against the current main HEAD is no longer authoritative for this mission because later non-mission commits are present on the branch; the audit had to reconstruct the effective mission commit set from progress_log.jsonl worker_completed commitIds.
Raw text grep over mission logs produces false hits from historical regex strings and audit notes, so the forbidden-verb check was narrowed to structured command fields / execute blocks only.

commands.command: git -C "/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/sanepi-mono" rev-parse HEAD && git -C "/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/sanepi-mono" merge-base HEAD main && git -C "/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/sanepi-mono" log --oneline --reverse 1677c199..HEAD
commands.observation: Confirmed the current branch HEAD is 75064599 and merge-base HEAD main also resolves to HEAD, while the broad 1677c199..HEAD log now includes later non-mission commits; this is why a mission-scoped reconstruction was required.

commands.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... reconstruct mission commitIds from progress_log.jsonl and classify each commit path against the updated allow-list ... PY
commands.observation: Reconstructed 17 mission commitIds, 44 unique touched paths, and 0 allow-list violations. Commit 619efcff is now correctly treated as compliant because the deleted legacy todowrite.ts path is explicitly allowed.

commands.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... structured scan of progress_log.jsonl handoff verification commands and worker-transcripts.jsonl Execute blocks for forbidden git verbs ... PY
commands.observation: Found 0 forbidden git-verb executions in actual command fields. The structured scan also retained an example of explicit path-based staging (`git add .factory/... && git commit ...`).
